title: Current architecture wins over superseded planning
goal: Prefer the active architecture decision over earlier drafts.

records.text: Initial thought: build the desktop shell before the memory engine so the UI can guide the API shape.
Decision: build the memory engine first. Desktop work stays downstream until the core retrieval and extraction behavior is stable.
The prior desktop-first idea is superseded. Current build order is femind first, then feloop.

retrieval_checks.query: What should be built before desktop work?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The memory engine should be built first.

retrieval_checks.query: Is desktop-first still the active plan?

title: Preference updates should replace stale preference answers
goal: Surface the current preference and retain change history.

records.text: For quick experiments, prefer local embeddings because they keep everything offline.
Update: for benchmark and evaluation workflows, prefer DeepInfra MiniLM embeddings because they are much faster and cheap enough.

retrieval_checks.query: What embedding path is preferred for evaluation workflows?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: DeepInfra MiniLM embeddings are preferred for evaluation workflows.

retrieval_checks.query: What was the earlier preference before that update?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The earlier preference was local embeddings for quick offline experiments.

title: Messy meeting notes should still produce usable extracted facts
goal: Extract decisions, blockers, and follow-up items from rough notes.

records.text: Wed sync notes. ANN looked good after rebuild fix. Still need live provider test. Keep DeepInfra for embeddings, try gpt-oss-120b first for extraction. Don't do large benchmark runs yet. Need to confirm stale facts lose to current facts before calling this production.

extraction_checks.expected_fact: ANN behavior improved after the rebuild fix.
DeepInfra should be used for embeddings.
gpt-oss-120b should be tried first for extraction.
Large benchmark runs should not happen yet.

retrieval_checks.query: What extraction model should be tried first?

title: Temporal questions should resolve to the current state
goal: Answer current-state questions correctly when the same field changed over time.

records.text: The repo is still named mindcore and has not been moved into the suite yet.
The repo is now fe-mind and the crate name is femind.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the current repo name?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The current repo name is fe-mind.

retrieval_checks.query: What was the earlier repo name?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The earlier repo name was mindcore.

title: Correct answer should survive similar distractor text
goal: Retrieve the right deployment detail even with similar noisy records nearby.

records.text: DeepInfra API key auth was verified for femind validation.
OpenRouter key remains available for unrelated experiments.
Anthropic CLI auth is configured locally but should not be used for the first DeepInfra validation pass.

retrieval_checks.query: Which provider auth was verified for the first femind validation pass?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: DeepInfra auth was verified for the first femind validation pass.

title: Linked supersession should resolve current and prior states cleanly
goal: Use explicit supersession links to answer current-state and historical-state questions over the same fact family.

records.text: The remote embedding service is hosted in WSL on calvaryav.
The remote embedding service is now hosted natively on Windows on calvaryav so the GPU path stays stable.
Mac clients still reach the service through an SSH tunnel to Windows localhost.

retrieval_checks.query: Where should the remote embedding service run now?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: It should run natively on Windows on calvaryav.

retrieval_checks.query: Where was the remote embedding service hosted before the native Windows move?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: It was hosted in WSL on calvaryav.

title: Aggregation queries should preserve broad provider coverage
goal: Return the full provider set for rollup questions instead of overfitting to a single top hit.

records.text: DeepInfra was evaluated as one extraction provider during the early smoke tests.
OpenRouter was also evaluated as an extraction provider during the comparison pass.
Anthropic was evaluated briefly as a third extraction provider before narrowing the lane.

retrieval_checks.query: How many providers were evaluated for extraction and which ones were they?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Three providers were evaluated for extraction: DeepInfra, OpenRouter, and Anthropic.

title: Graph-linked retrieval should bridge client, tunnel, and host notes
goal: Answer a connectivity question that spans several linked records rather than one self-contained note.

records.text: Librona now verifies against the FeMind remote embedding service instead of its earlier service assumptions.
The stable GPU embedding path now comes from hosting the FeMind remote service natively on Windows on calvaryav.
Mac clients reach it through an SSH tunnel to Windows localhost on calvaryav.

retrieval_checks.query: How does Librona reach the stable GPU embedding service now?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: It reaches the service through an SSH tunnel to Windows localhost on calvaryav.

title: Exact artifact details should be grounded and unsupported identifiers should abstain
goal: Return documented local artifact details while refusing exact identifiers that were never recorded.

records.text: The stable FeMind tunnel helper lives at /Users/johndeaton/bin/femind-remote-on.
The launchd agent file that keeps the FeMind tunnel alive is /Users/johndeaton/Library/LaunchAgents/com.user.femind-embed-tunnel.plist.

retrieval_checks.query: Which launchd plist keeps the FeMind tunnel alive?

abstention_checks.query: What exact scheduled task GUID keeps the same tunnel alive on Windows?

title: System should abstain when the answer is not present
goal: Avoid fabrication when the source records never contained the answer.

records.text: Need to compare extraction quality between gpt-oss-120b and Nemotron after the smoke test.
Do not run large benchmark sweeps before the practical eval set looks good.

abstention_checks.query: What was the exact total token cost of the last Nemotron run?
